steps.title: Identify what changes between the two sets
steps.content: Maya examined 78 plants with masses in the range [3.1 kg, 7.4 kg]. Omar examined the same 78 plants plus one additional plant weighing 12.6 kg.

steps.title: Determine how the mean changes
steps.content:
Since all of Maya's plants are in the range [3.1, 7.4] kg, the mean of Maya's set is at most 7.4 kg.

The new plant weighs 12.6 kg, which is greater than 7.4 kg and therefore greater than any possible mean of Maya's original set.

When you add a value above the current mean to a data set, the mean increases.

Therefore, Omar's mean must be greater than Maya's mean. That matches Choice D — The mean of the masses, in kg, of the plants that Maya examined is less than the mean of the masses, in kg, of the plants that Omar examined..

steps.title: Check the range distractor
steps.content: Maya's range is $7.4-3.1=4.3$ kg. Omar adds a plant weighing $12.6$ kg, so Omar's range is $12.6-3.1=9.5$ kg. Maya's range is not greater than Omar's range, so Choice B is false.
